reforms.description: The Australian Government announced a new investment allowance for new plant and equipment ordered on or after 1st January 1976, aimed at stimulating investment across all industries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment allowance is designed to incentivize firms to invest in new equipment, which can enhance productivity and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Treasurer released details of the new investment allowance.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the investment landscape, encouraging capital investment which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The allowance will apply to new plant and equipment ordered on or after 1st January 1976 and by 30th June 1978 at a rate of 40 percent.
